chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse

parts.text: أَبِي بَصِيرٍ : أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَرِضَ فَأَمَرَ غِلْمَانَهُ أَنْ يَحْمِلُوهُ
يَطُوفُوا بِهِ فَأَمَرَهُمْ أَنْ يُخَطُّوا بِرِجْلِهِ اَلْأَرْضَ حَتَّى تَمَسَّ اَلْأَرْضَ قَدَمَاهُ

text:
اَلطَّوَافِ.
وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنِ اَلرَّبِيعِ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ : أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ كُلَّمَا بَلَغَ إِلَى اَلرُّكْنِ اَلْيَمَانِيِّ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2820 - It is narrated from Abu Basir:
"Imam Abu Abdullah (as) fell ill, so he instructed his attendants to carry him and circumambulate the Kaaba with him. He ordered them to drag his foot on the ground so that his feet would touch the ground during the circumambulation."
And in another narration by Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl from Al-Rabi ibn Khuthaym:
"He would do this whenever he reached the Yemeni Corner (al-Rukn al-Yamani)."
